title: symphytum sylvaticum var. hordokopii
intro: Symphytum sylvaticum var. hordokopii, also known as Alant, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Alant include relieving respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Alant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to soothe and protect the skin, as well as to calm respiratory issues.
